summary: TestRateUnitPrefixed is a correct byte-identity anchor: 8 cases (disk/net x r/w x maxFit/maxFit+1) assert the FULL string field+" "+prefix+unit with hardcoded literals (not a call to the deleted rateField), locking the space separator, the r/w prefix, the 4-digit reserve padding, and the promotion boundary where an off-by-one would hide. The want values are non-circular hardcoded strings. Existing TestRateUnit/_boundary/_property are unchanged and still lock RateUnit's no-space form. The top/stat_test.go verbose goldens (1135 rMB/s, 1546 wMB/s, 4345 rMbps, 6543 wMbps) are unchanged and NOT in this commit (confirmed via git show --stat), proving byte-identity through delegation at the real call sites with mid-range non-boundary values. rateField is fully deleted from top/stat.go. go test ./internal/pretty/... ./top/... is green. Coverage is sufficient to prove the refactor is byte-identical; the two unexercised-via-prefixed-form regimes (unknown-family default, widen-beyond-reserve) are already locked for the shared rateUnitParts core through the unchanged RateUnit suite, so they are minor/advisory, not correctness gaps.

findings.issue: TestRateUnitPrefixed exercises only FamilyDisk and FamilyNet. The unknown/empty-family default branch (falls back to MB/s) is not driven through RateUnitPrefixed. This is the only family path not asserted in the prefixed form.
findings.suggestion: Optional: add one case {name: "unknown family defaults to disk", v: 50.0, family: "", prefix: "r", width: 4, want: "  50 rMB/s"}. Not required for correctness: the family->unit selection lives in the shared rateUnitParts core and is already locked byte-identical by TestRateUnit (line 108), and the prefix/space wrapping is family-independent. Captured as advisory only.

findings.issue: The anchor table tests only the two boundary inputs (maxFit=9999, maxFit+1=10000). A mid-range non-boundary value and the widen-beyond-reserve regime are not asserted directly on RateUnitPrefixed.
findings.suggestion: No change required. The mid-range case is already covered at the real call sites by the unchanged top/stat_test.go goldens (1135/1546 rMB-s, 4345/6543 rMbps); the widen-beyond-reserve regime is locked for the shared core by TestRateUnit_property. The boundary table deliberately and correctly targets the exact promotion boundary where a refactor off-by-one would hide. Advisory only.
